description: Produce a single HTML page that dynamically fetches and parses a YAML file at runtime (via a fetch request to a local file or endpoint) structured by store, then section, then item. The page renders a grocery list organized by store and by section within each store, with an 'Any Store' category for unassigned items. Each item has a checkbox to mark it as purchased. The page includes a Submit button that POSTs the full grocery list as JSON to http://localhost:3000/grocery-list, and a Clear button that unchecks all checkboxes. The stores are Metro, Food Basics, and Costco. The list is intended for 4 people covering weeknight dinners (pasta, chicken stir fry, tacos).
constraints: YAML file is fetched and parsed at runtime in the browser
YAML structure is: store > section > items
Unassigned items fall under a section called 'Any Store'
Each item must have a checkbox for marking as purchased
Submit button POSTs the grocery list as JSON to http://localhost:3000/grocery-list
Clear button unchecks all checkboxes on the page
Stores displayed: Metro, Food Basics, Costco, and Any Store
Page must include a YAML parsing library (e.g., js-yaml via CDN) to parse the fetched YAML at runtime
